title: implement-vso-jwt-auth-setup
summary: Implemented `scripts/configure-vso-jwt-auth.sh` (idempotent Vault JWT/OIDC auth setup for VSO, using jwks_url+bound_issuer per the Phase 1 spike decision, strict bound_audiences/bound_subject role binding, no token_reviewer_jwt) plus a 20-assertion validation test suite, and verified live against the real two-cluster lab (idempotency, correct login, wrong-audience/wrong-SA rejections).
keyLearnings: - - Critical gotcha found during live e2e testing: `vault read auth/.../config` in plain-text table format wraps multi-line PEM values (jwks_ca_pem) across many lines with no per-line key prefix on continuation lines — a naive `grep -vE '^jwks_ca_pem'` filter only strips the first line and leaks the rest of the CA cert. Fixed by using `vault read -format=json | jq 'del(.data.jwks_ca_pem, .data.jwt_validation_pubkeys, .data.oidc_discovery_ca_pem)'` instead, which added `jq` to the script's required commands. Any future script reading back JWT/OIDC config for display must follow this pattern.

- - Live e2e proof captured: correct `vso-demo` JWT (aud=vault) login succeeds and returns `["default" "mysecret"]` policies; `--audience not-vault` fails with "invalid audience (aud) claim"; `kubectl create token default -n vso-demo` (wrong SA) fails with "invalid subject (sub) claim"; `vault auth list` shows `jwt-vso/` and `kubernetes-vso/` coexisting without interference.

- - Test-writing gotcha: static "script never writes X" grep assertions must exclude the script's own explanatory comments/completion-message echoes (which legitimately mention `token_reviewer_jwt`/`oidc_discovery_url` to explain their absence) — match on the actual `key=value` write-argument pattern, not a bare substring.

- - Deliberately left the `oidc-discovery-reader` ClusterRole/ClusterRoleBinding formalization (flagged by task 01's spike as needed in "task 04 or 05") to task 05 (`scripts/setup-vso-cluster.sh`), since task 05's own spec is scoped to that exact file and task 04's spec/steps never mention RBAC. It remains applied out-of-band on the live cluster (confirmed present) but is not yet in any script — task 05 must add it.
- - Downstream tasks 05-08 (setup-vso-cluster.sh reviewer removal, VaultAuth method:jwt switch, Makefile targets, verify-two-cluster.sh JWT checks) are all still pending and untouched by this task.
blockers: none — all acceptance/validation criteria for task 04 met and re-verified live (bash -n
role binds exact vso-demo/vso-demo subject + vault audience
